evals.prompt: I've got a fresh GPU box spun up in the cloud and I want to run NemoClaw on it so my agent can actually use the GPU. What's the right way to get it running end-to-end on that remote host?
evals.expected_output: Recommend provisioning the VM, running the standard NemoClaw installer on the remote host, then running `nemoclaw onboard` on that host; describe how to connect into the sandbox afterward and note the dashboard origin setting when reaching it from a remote browser.
assertions: Response recommends running the standard NemoClaw installer directly on the remote GPU host rather than only on a local machine
Response describes running `nemoclaw onboard` on the remote host to configure the gateway, providers, and sandbox
Response explains how to connect into the sandbox afterward (for example `nemoclaw <name> connect`, SSH plus `openclaw tui`, or `openshell term`)
Response mentions that `CHAT_UI_URL` must be set to the origin the browser will use before setup when the dashboard is accessed from a remote URL
Response does NOT present `nemoclaw deploy <instance-name>` as the preferred or current path
Response does NOT tell the user that installing NemoClaw only on their laptop is enough to run it on the remote GPU

evals.prompt: I'm using the older `nemoclaw deploy my-gpu-box` wrapper because it's what our team scripts still use. It provisions the VM but onboarding complains the GPU isn't detected, and I also can't figure out how to SSH in with the name it printed. What's going on and what should I check?
evals.expected_output: Explain the legacy Brev compatibility path, how the deploy wrapper installs Docker and the NVIDIA Container Toolkit only when a GPU is present, how to override GPU selection with `NEMOCLAW_GPU` (and provider with `NEMOCLAW_BREV_PROVIDER`), and how Brev sets up SSH config for the instance name; note the command is deprecated.
assertions: Response acknowledges that `nemoclaw deploy` is a deprecated compatibility path, not the preferred flow
Response explains that the deploy wrapper only installs the NVIDIA Container Toolkit when a GPU is detected on the host
Response mentions the `NEMOCLAW_GPU` environment variable (or `NEMOCLAW_BREV_PROVIDER`) as a way to change GPU selection or Brev provider before running deploy
Response points to Brev-managed SSH configuration (for example `ssh <instance-name>`) as how to reach the host by name
Response suggests re-running `nemoclaw onboard` on the host once the GPU is visible, rather than starting over from scratch
Response does NOT claim that `nemoclaw deploy` has been fully removed or is completely unusable

evals.prompt: I just got a new MacBook and I want to try NemoClaw for the first time. What do I need to do to get it installed and running so I can chat with an agent?
evals.expected_output: Recognize this is a first-time local install on a laptop, not a remote GPU deployment; redirect toward the standard getting-started flow (installer plus onboarding wizard plus first prompt on the local machine).
assertions: Response identifies this as a first-time local install on a laptop, not a remote GPU deployment
Response points to the standard NemoClaw installer and `nemoclaw onboard` flow on the local machine
Response does NOT walk the user through `nemoclaw deploy <instance-name>` or the legacy Brev compatibility path
Response does NOT tell the user they must provision a remote GPU instance to try NemoClaw
Response does NOT recommend setting `CHAT_UI_URL`, `NEMOCLAW_GPU`, or other remote-deployment environment variables for a local laptop install
Response redirects toward the first-time getting-started journey rather than the remote deploy journey
